PAIN.quote: My driver swing speed is 100mph on avg but hitting the driver well is the biggest struggle of my game right now. Depending on the course I may have 1/2 dozen punch out shots from bad drives
PAIN.context: High swing speed player still struggling with driver

PAIN.quote: I have many many many...many drivers varying in all types all lengths. You name it & I probably have it or had it. Still looking for the 'One'
PAIN.context: Endless driver search without finding solution

PAIN.quote: My problem is not a slice, but it is more about consistent middle of the club strike
I do struggle with the driver. I'm sure I'm a little over the top as I tend to cast a bit with longer clubs
I have been sending e-mails to the support people for the GX7 but they seem to be ignoring me. I have confirmed that I have been charged for the club on my credit card and that's all!

HOPE.quote: I recently purchased the Hammer X Driver w/Aerospeed shaft and am amazed at the power and accuracy it produces. I've tried every big name driver on the planet and none of them comes close
I'm actually picking up an extra 15-20 yards with the Hammer X Driver
HOPE.context: Distance gain claim from infomercial driver

HOPE.quote: The idea may help many of us that don't have higher club head speed. The length alone may prove a it's biggest feature
HOPE.context: Shorter shaft concept seen as potentially beneficial

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The driver is the most unforgiving club in the bag due to its lower loft and longer shaft. Lower loft creates more sidespin when the ball is cut
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Technical explanation of why driver is hardest club

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I'm betting that, in the hands of anyone with a SS over 100mph, this thing would be the most devastating hook machine ever
High SS players should not apply. But the guy does admit it's not for those types of players from the very beginning
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Infomercial drivers designed for slower swingers only

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I am interested in the concept of taking an inch or so off my shaft length
The best driver for me this year was an old Ping Rapture V2. Hit it better than the new Ping G series driver
With that swing speed you might benefit using a shorter shaft on your driver without the added loft

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Smaller head, extreme heel/toe and back weighting, Hot face, higher loft and reduced turf interaction. Looks like it would work well for their target customer which is the senior/am with lower SS
Looks like a 3 wood to me. 14*/43''

According to the website, a person is supposed to drive the ball 50 yards farther. The zero cc see-thru design is suppose to increase swing speed. The sword-like X-shaft cuts through the air for a 20% increase in swing speed
Cut an inch of your driver. Put some lead tape in the heel to swingweight and create some draw bias. Boom. Maxvert
You can buy a good condition used G425 SFT for less than the price of the Maxvert
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Big Golf draw driver cheaper than infomercial

VILLAIN.quote: This is a up at 2am watching Golf Channel kinda driver
They're desperate, they're more willing to buy this than invest time and money into lessons and practice
Why do people even bother with this crap? Go find a used Cobra Max or Cleveland 12* offset driver for 1/2 the price of this, cut it down to 44* and go have fun

VILLAIN.context: Suspicion that positive reviews are fake/bots

VILLAIN.quote: Only 2 post and both about the Hammer?
We a gonna see these guys eventually on american greed. The FTC has outlawed puffery like this. Report these guys
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver

VILLAIN.quote: The swingweight was closer to H-0 than it was to D-1, the 10.5 degree behemoth launched lower and with less spin than my 7.5 910D2 set to 6.75*
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver
VILLAIN.context: Hammer X technical specs are terrible

VILLAIN.quote: It feels as bad as it looks, and it looks horrible. Somehow I did hit it straight, might've been the 18* launch and 5500rpm that wouldn't let the ball move
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver
VILLAIN.context: Hammer X quality and design criticized

VILLAIN.quote: I highly recommend saving your money by not purchasing this club
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver

VILLAIN.quote: Yes it works! To the complainers... It's always the worst hacks that write these negative reviews who have nothing better to do
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver

VILLAIN.quote: Any weekend golfer would rather pay $99 over $500 for the same performance! Be wise!
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver
VILLAIN.context: Infomercial price appeal to value shoppers

sources_covered: GolfWRX - Has anyone hit the Hammer X driver
high_value_themes_identified: Infomercial products widely ridiculed on forums
Positive reviews suspected of being bots/shills
Legitimate Big Golf draw drivers cheaper than infomercials
Customer service issues with infomercial companies
Technical specs on infomercial clubs often poor
